questions.q: In which year did India undertake its major economic liberalisation reforms described here?
questions.explanation: India undertook major economic liberalisation reforms in 1991.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms were introduced amid which kind of crisis?
questions.explanation: The reforms were introduced amid a balance-of-payments crisis.

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister of India during the 1991 economic reforms?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao was the Prime Minister who presided over the 1991 economic liberalisation.

questions.q: Who was the Finance Minister who led the 1991 economic reforms?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh was the Finance Minister who led the 1991 economic reforms.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms opened the Indian economy to what?
questions.explanation: The reforms opened the economy to private and foreign investment.

questions.q: A central aim of the 1991 reforms was to reduce what?
options: The size of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The reforms aimed to reduce state controls over the economy.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms liberalised which of the following?
questions.explanation: The reforms liberalised trade, among other measures.

questions.q: In which decade did the economic liberalisation described here begin?
questions.explanation: The economic liberalisation began in 1991, within the 1990s.

questions.q: The system of industrial licensing that the 1991 reforms largely dismantled was popularly known by what name?
questions.explanation: The pre-1991 system of industrial licensing was popularly called the 'Licence Raj.'

questions.q: The 1991 reforms are generally described as moving India toward what kind of economy?
questions.explanation: The reforms moved India toward a more market-oriented and open economy.

questions.q: The Prime Minister during the 1991 reforms, P.V. Narasimha Rao, was distinguished as the first Prime Minister from which region to serve a full term?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao was the first Prime Minister from South India to serve a full term.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms included a significant adjustment of the Indian rupee's value, known as what?
questions.explanation: The 1991 reforms included a devaluation of the rupee to address the external crisis.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms substantially abolished the requirement of government licences for most industries; this is often summarised as what?
questions.explanation: The reforms involved delicensing, removing licensing requirements for most industries.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh, who led the 1991 reforms as Finance Minister, later held which office from 2004 to 2014?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh served as Prime Minister of India from 2004 to 2014.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms reduced barriers to foreign capital, commonly measured as which type of investment inflow?
questions.explanation: The reforms eased restrictions on Foreign Direct Investment into India.

questions.q: The 1991 balance-of-payments crisis involved a sharp fall in which key external resource?
questions.explanation: The crisis involved a sharp depletion of India's foreign exchange reserves.

questions.q: Which institution was approached for financial assistance during the 1991 crisis?
questions.explanation: India approached the International Monetary Fund for assistance during the 1991 crisis.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms were presented to Parliament most prominently through which annual statement?
questions.explanation: The reforms were prominently articulated through the Union Budget of 1991.

questions.q: The reduction of tariffs under the 1991 reforms was aimed at making Indian trade more what?
questions.explanation: Lowering tariffs aimed to open Indian trade to greater international competition.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms are commonly described using which trio of terms?
questions.explanation: The reforms are commonly summarised as liberalisation, privatisation and globalisation.

questions.q: Before 1991, India's economic framework had been guided largely by which planning approach?
options: Five-Year Plans under the Planning Commission
questions.explanation: Before 1991, India's economy was guided largely by Five-Year Plans under the Planning Commission.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao led the government that introduced the 1991 reforms after which general election?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao became Prime Minister after the 1991 general election.

questions.q: The 1991 New Industrial Policy abolished industrial licensing for most industries but retained it for a small list of sectors related to what consideration?
questions.explanation: Licensing was retained only for a short list of industries linked to strategic, security and environmental concerns.

questions.q: As part of the 1991 crisis management, India reportedly pledged a quantity of which asset to raise short-term foreign exchange?
questions.explanation: India pledged part of its gold reserves to raise short-term foreign exchange during the 1991 crisis.

questions.q: A committee on financial sector reforms set up around this period was chaired by whom?
questions.explanation: The Narasimham Committee examined and recommended reforms to the banking and financial sector.

questions.q: The market regulator strengthened in the early 1990s reform period to oversee securities markets is known by which acronym?
questions.explanation: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) was given statutory powers in 1992 to regulate securities markets.

questions.q: The pre-1991 trade regime that the reforms dismantled relied heavily on which kind of quantitative restriction on imports?
questions.explanation: The pre-1991 regime relied on import quotas and licensing, which the reforms progressively dismantled.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms moved the rupee toward greater convertibility, beginning notably on which account?
questions.explanation: The reforms moved first toward current account convertibility of the rupee in the years following 1991.

questions.q: The institution responsible for monetary policy that played a key role in managing the 1991 crisis is which body?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India, as the central bank, played a key role in managing the 1991 crisis.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms are widely regarded as a turning point because they shifted policy away from which earlier orientation?
questions.explanation: The reforms marked a shift away from a largely inward-looking, state-led economic model.
